name: Electric Machinery Employees
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union in Minneapolis offering checking, savings, and loan services to 780 members with $8.54M in assets.
description_long:
Electric Machinery Employees Credit Union is a small, community-focused financial institution headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Established in 1946, the credit union has operated for 80 years as a federally insured state-chartered institution, serving employees and members in the local area. The organization maintains a single branch location at 800 Central Ave NE in Minneapolis and is governed by leadership including CEO Greg D Ericson and Chairperson Richard Gross.

The credit union offers traditional banking and financial services including checking accounts, savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and other lending products totaling approximately $4.06 million. Members have access to deposit accounts, with regular shares and other deposit products comprising their funding base.

As a state-chartered natural person credit union with 780 members and $8.54 million in total assets, Electric Machinery Employees Credit Union distinguishes itself as a locally-focused institution serving a specific membership base. The organization is not part of the Federal Home Loan Bank system and does not carry a low-income credit union designation, positioning it as a standard community credit union serving its defined membership.

The institution's most recent audit was completed December 1, 2024. However, the latest financial data shows the credit union reported a net loss of $29,809, indicating operational challenges. With total liabilities of $6.45 million against equity of $2.08 million, the organization maintains a modest equity cushion. Prospective members should note the credit union's small size and single-branch operation may limit service convenience compared to larger regional or national institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Federally insured deposit protection through NCUSIF
80-year operating history demonstrates stability and longevity
Community-focused local credit union serving Minneapolis members
Offers multiple loan products including real estate and unsecured loans
ATM and debit card access for member convenience
cons: Very small institution with only 1 branch location limits accessibility
Recent financial loss ($29,809 net loss) indicates operational stress
Minimal total assets ($8.54M) restrict lending capacity and service depth
Small membership base (780 members) may limit product offerings and competitive rates
best_for: Local Minneapolis area residents seeking community-based banking relationships
Individuals with existing membership or employee ties to the organization
Members prioritizing personal service from a small, established credit union
diagnosis: Electric Machinery Employees Credit Union is best for existing members or Minneapolis-area residents with connections to the organization seeking traditional banking services from an established community institution. The primary caveat is the organization's small size (single branch, $8.54M assets, recent operating losses) which may limit service options, product depth, and competitive rates compared to larger financial institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
